text: fat is 1.3g. omega 3 is doing serious work here. for a grain, this is really about carb behaviour.
carbs is 75.9g. the fibre support does a lot of the heavy lifting. for grains, the real question is whether those carbs come with enough fibre and stability.
protein is 12.3g. protein quality is clearly one of the selling points. protein helps, but grains still need the carb side to stay under control.
vitamin e at 0% DV stand out most. that is not enough to move the verdict very far on its own.
magnesium at 41% DV, iron at 14% DV, zinc at 13% DV stand out most. that is useful, but still not a huge mineral edge.

on the plus side. useful staple energy source because that matters because grains are often judged by how practical they are in real diets. works as a meal anchor because a lot of value comes from being easy to build around. usually affordable and scalable because practicality matters for staple foods.

the drawbacks next. protein quality is limited because even the better grains usually need pairing. carb load is still the main burden because that is the category risk no grain fully escapes. quality can drop fast with processing because the grain category punishes refinement pretty hard.
Bulgur is A tier. Best when you want a staple carb that actually brings something useful with it.
